problem_statement: The paper addresses how to identify, evaluate, and mitigate cybersecurity risks (injection, insecure templates, backdoors, adversarial manipulation) introduced by Generative-AI automatic code generation, which are exacerbated by a lack of human oversight.
magnitude_evidence: Cybersecurity risk impact levels averaged 74.43% (MLR) and 76.0% (real-world study) across 14 risk categories, with r=0.94 correlation; ANN normalized importance ranked Injection Attacks highest at 100%, Privacy Issues/Data Leakage 71.2%, Insufficient Logging/Monitoring 63.3%.
insufficiency_evidence: "lack of human oversight" lets Generative AI produce inadvertently insecure code; static analysis tools "provide static security vulnerability analysis... however, not all vulnerabilities raised... even if they have not detected dynamic flaws"; manual code reviews/penetration testing are "time-consuming and not practical for large-scale projects... procedure-based testing quickly becomes overwhelmed by the sheer amount of code"; black-box models "risk generating exploitative output without recognizing how individual vulnerabilities... are being injected".
notes: Text contains internal inconsistencies in reported ANN configuration (hidden neurons stated as 4, then 10; dataset N=350 vs 70 survey respondents; epochs 50 vs 100; splits 70/30 vs 80/20) and some garbled/typo-laden passages; focus is cybersecurity of AI code generation rather than general oversight-scaling.
